topic: Eagles draft analysis — need a cornerback

turns.content: draft is next week. tell me the Eagles aren't going to screw this up.
The draft is always stressful for a dedicated fan. What's your biggest concern — are you worried about them reaching for the wrong position, or about the front office trading picks again?
cornerback. we need a corner and everybody in the building knows it. secondary got torched all year. if they draft another linebacker or some project receiver I'm going to lose it.

You're not wrong — the secondary was a clear weak spot. There are some solid cornerback prospects in this draft class. Are you hoping they address it in the first round, or would you be okay with them taking best player available early and getting a corner in the second?
first round. period. you don't fix a house fire by putting it off till tomorrow. you go now. same thing here. take the corner at 18 and stop overthinking it.

Ha — fair analogy. If you're picking at 18, there should be one or two quality corners still on the board at that spot. Anyone you've got your eye on specifically, or are you just hoping they take whichever corner is the best available when their number comes up?

there's a kid out of Ohio State. athletic, physical, plays press man. that's what we need. none of these soft zone corners who give up 10 yards of cushion on every snap. give me a guy who wants to get in somebody's face.

A physical press-man corner would fit the Eagles' defensive identity well. Ohio State has been producing NFL-ready defensive backs pretty consistently. The question is whether he'll still be there at 18 — corners like that tend to go fast. If he's gone, is there a backup plan you'd be okay with?

there's a couple guys from the SEC that could work. but my point is don't get cute. Howie loves to trade back and accumulate picks like he's playing fantasy football. sometimes you just take the guy. pick is in. done. move on.

That's a fair criticism. Trading back can work, but it can also mean watching the player you wanted walk to a division rival. Especially at a position of need like corner — there are only so many in any class who can step in and play right away. What's your read on the rest of the NFC East heading into the draft?

Dallas is a mess and I enjoy that. Giants are rebuilding for the fourth year in a row. Washington is the only one that worries me a little. but listen. it all comes down to what we do. take care of your own house. draft the corner. build the secondary. the rest takes care of itself.
Can't argue with that — control what you can control and let the rest sort itself out. Are you doing anything special for draft night, or just watching from home?
couple of the guys are coming over. beer and wings. we do it every year. phone goes off, nobody talks except to yell at the TV. it's a good time.
That sounds like the right way to do it. Beer, wings, and yelling at the TV — no notes. When the pick comes in and it's a corner, you can take a victory lap.
and when it's a punter I'm throwing a wing at the screen. either way it's entertainment.
Ha — punter at 18 would be a new low even for the NFC East. Enjoy draft night. Hopefully you're celebrating, not throwing food.
